reason: The prompt asked to "write a gentle apology," and Response A actually does this with warm, concise, second-person language that genuine Claude models produce when following such an instruction. Response B is a meta-description of an apology ("I have written a gentle apology... The apology acknowledges...") rather than the apology itself, which is a classic instruction-following failure an imitator would produce, not authentic Haiku behavior. Haiku follows the literal directive to write the apology.
